title: Jennifer Lawrence’s ‘Red Sparrow’ Transformation With Francis Lawrence | The Big Picture (Ep. 53)
format: Sean Fennessey talks to director Francis Lawrence about Red Sparrow, his fourth film with Jennifer Lawrence.

films.title: Red Sparrow
films.note: The subject. A hard-R spy thriller adapted from ex-CIA officer Jason Matthews's novel; the Ringer office took to calling it 'sex spies.' Lawrence pitched studio president Emma Watts on an R rating over the phone and changed the book's ending. He pushes back on calling it an action movie, saying that's a common misread.

films.title: The Hunger Games: Catching Fire
films.note: The first of his films with Lawrence, a role he inherited coming onto the series' second installment.

films.title: The Hunger Games: Mockingjay - Part 1
films.note: Part of the roughly five straight years he spent inside the franchise.

films.title: The Hunger Games: Mockingjay - Part 2
films.note: He was in post on the last Mockingjay when Fox sent him the Red Sparrow book.

films.title: I Am Legend
films.note: His example of not assuming an audience knows anything: a quarter of critics thought he'd left the city's power on because he never showed Will Smith kick-starting the pantry generators.

films.title: There Will Be Blood
films.note: His stated favorite film of all time; he'd caught it on TV the day before and sat through the whole thing again when asked for the last great thing he'd seen.

films.title: Phantom Thread
films.note: His pick for a recent great, another Paul Thomas Anderson film, a director he says inspires him.

referenced.group: R-rated studio hits cited to the studio

In development (unmade): Women's Battalion of Death (Maria Bochkareva project)
Untitled Steve Knight sci-fi series for Apple
